seoTitle: Auto Assign Tickets in Zendesk: 2026 Trigger Setup
metaDescription: Learn how to auto assign tickets in Zendesk using 2026 Admin Center triggers, condition rules, and capacity-based routing so no inquiry sits unassigned.
intro: Manually sorting every incoming support request wastes agent time and delays first responses. This guide walks through setting up an automated ticket routing rule in Zendesk's Admin Center so new, unassigned tickets get matched to the right group or agent the moment they arrive. You'll come away with a working trigger that fires on ticket creation and applies your chosen routing logic.

steps.heading: Open the Triggers panel
steps.body: Go to Admin Center, expand Objects and Rules, and select Triggers from the sidebar. This is where all automated ticket actions in Zendesk live.

steps.heading: Create a new trigger
steps.body: Click Create Trigger and give it a clear, descriptive name and category so you can find it later among your other routing rules.

steps.heading: Set the conditions
steps.body: Under Meet All conditions, add rules such as Ticket is Created and Assignee is Unassigned, plus any channel or brand conditions you need.

steps.heading: Define the routing action
steps.body: In the Actions panel, choose to set the ticket's Group or Assignee based on your team structure, and consider adding a triage tag so the rule doesn't re-fire.

steps.heading: Enable omnichannel capacity rules
steps.body: If you're using Zendesk's omnichannel routing, review capacity settings so tickets balance across available agents instead of overloading one person.

steps.heading: Save and test
steps.body: Save the trigger globally, then submit a test ticket and check the event timeline to confirm the automation fired correctly.

faq.question: Will this affect tickets that are already assigned?
faq.answer: No, the condition requiring the assignee to be unassigned means the trigger only acts on fresh, unrouted tickets.

faq.question: Can I route based on skills instead of just groups?
faq.answer: Yes, Zendesk's omnichannel capacity engine supports skill-based queues alongside group and agent assignment.

faq.question: Do I need admin access to set this up?
faq.answer: Yes, creating and editing triggers requires Zendesk Admin Center access.

faq.question: How do I check if the trigger is actually working?
faq.answer: Open an affected ticket and review its event timeline, which shows exactly which trigger fired and what action it took.
